query: As of early July 2026, what percentage of Russia's national oil-refining capacity is offline/disabled due to Ukrainian drone strikes?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the findings:

**As of early July 2026, the answer depends on the source and metric:**

- **Ukrainian official claim (headline figure):** The General Staff of Ukraine's Armed Forces stated (July 4, 2026, via Kyiv Post) that strikes have **disabled 42.7% of Russia's total oil refining capacity**, with $13.5B in cumulative losses since August 2025.

- **Independent estimates are lower** but still severe: Carnegie's expert analysis (June 2026) put capacity loss at up to **~28%**, and Reuters/Moscow Times (May 20, 2026) at **~25%** (83 million metric tons/year of capacity halted or sharply reduced).

**Key caveats I flagged:**
1. The 42.7% is a Ukrainian government claim; independent analysts cluster around 25–28%.
2. The figure is a moving target — refineries are repeatedly struck, partially repaired, and struck again.
3. There's an important distinction between **refining** capacity (~25–43%) and **export** capacity (~40% halted at one point in March 2026) — these are often conflated.

Supporting facts: fuel production down 25% YoY in June, production ~20% below domestic demand, >30% of gasoline output affected, the Moscow/Kapotnya refinery out until 2027, and Russia banning gasoline exports with rationing in 50+ regions.

Full structured output with all sources and data points has been submitted.

sources.title: Ukrainian Defense Forces Disable 43% of Russia's Total Oil Refining Capacity
sources.snippet: General Staff of Ukraine announced strikes have disabled 42.7% of Russia's total oil refining capacity, $13.5B in losses since August 2025.

sources.title: Russian Oil Sector Battered but Not Broken by Ukrainian Air Attacks
sources.snippet: Moscow/TANECO strikes knocked out ~600,000 bbl/day; capacity loss might be 28 percent down on previous years.

sources.title: Drone Strikes Force Central Russian Refineries to Halt or Cut Output – Reuters
sources.snippet: Refineries with combined capacity exceeding 83M metric tons/year (~25% of total) halted or sharply reduced; >30% gasoline, ~25% diesel output affected.

sources.title: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Halt at Least 40% of Russia's Oil Export Capacity – Reuters
sources.snippet: ~40% of Russia's oil export capacity knocked out; most severe supply disruption in modern Russian history.

sources.title: Moscow Refinery Unlikely To Resume Operations After Drone Strikes Until 2027 — Reuters
sources.snippet: Moscow (Kapotnya) refinery expected offline at least six months, unlikely to resume until 2027.

sources.title: Russia's fuel crisis deepens following Ukrainian drone strikes
sources.snippet: Drone strikes triggered fuel shortages, rationing, queues, and export bans across Russia.

sources.title: IEA Sees Drone Hits Weighing on Russia Refinery Runs to Mid-2026
sources.snippet: IEA projected drone strikes would suppress Russian refinery processing rates until at least mid-2026.

sources.title: Ukrainian drone attacks on oil refineries plunge Russia into a summer fuel crisis
sources.snippet: Months of Ukrainian attacks set refineries ablaze, choking fuel supplies and forcing rationing.

data_points.metric: Share of Russia's total oil refining capacity disabled (Ukrainian claim)
Cumulative Russian refining industry losses since Aug 2025
Barrels/day knocked out by mid-June Moscow + TANECO strikes
Share of total refining capacity halted/reduced (Reuters)
Annual capacity of refineries halted or significantly reduced
Share of Russian gasoline production affected
Share of Russian oil EXPORT capacity halted (distinct from refining)
Refined output decline from peak (~5.2M bbl/day baseline)
